sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_sizes (id UInt32, size_bytes UInt64) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT id, size_bytes, formatReadableDecimalSize(size_bytes) AS readable_size FROM test_sizes WHERE size_bytes > 0;
SELECT id, size_bytes, formatReadableDecimalSize(size_bytes) AS readable_size FROM test_sizes WHERE readable_size IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, size_bytes, formatReadableDecimalSize(size_bytes) AS readable_size FROM test_sizes WHERE (size_bytes > 1024) AND (size_bytes < 2147483648);
SELECT id, size_bytes, formatReadableDecimalSize(IF(size_bytes IS NULL, 0, size_bytes)) AS readable_size FROM test_sizes;
CREATE TABLE test_sizes_extended AS test_sizes; ALTER TABLE test_sizes_extended ADD COLUMN extra_bytes Nullable(UInt64);
UPDATE test_sizes_extended SET extra_bytes = 5000 WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, formatReadableDecimalSize(size_bytes + IFNULL(extra_bytes, 0)) AS total_readable FROM test_sizes_extended;
SELECT id, size_bytes, formatReadableDecimalSize(size_bytes) AS readable_size FROM (SELECT id, size_bytes FROM test_sizes) AS subquery WHERE readable_size > '0 KB';
SELECT id, size_bytes FROM test_sizes WHERE FORMATReadableDecimalSize(size_bytes) > '1 KB';
SELECT id, count() AS count_sizes FROM test_sizes GROUP BY readable_size HAVING count_sizes > 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT formatReadableDecimalSize(size_bytes) AS distinct_sizes FROM test_sizes);
SELECT id, size_bytes, formatReadableDecimalSize(size_bytes) AS readable FROM test_sizes WHERE size_bytes IS NOT NULL AND size_bytes > 1024 ORDER BY readable DESC;
